session.name: MPI-165 Phase A SHIPPED + live-verified both engines (loader-swap + media-stamp converter was the REAL Pod-reject fix) + Preview_Only tier-2 noise cleaned. Resume at Phase B (profile migration + one resolver — offline, parity-tested, NO Pod needed to start).

goal.original: Fix the half-wired LTX bf16-local/GGUF-Pod engine split so a fresh GGUF-only Pod installs + generates correctly; then consolidate the engine-variant smear into ONE clean structure (engines: profile + one resolver + resolve-engine-once) rather than keep symptom-patching.
goal.summary: Phase A is DONE + LIVE-VERIFIED on both engines (local bf16 RTX 4060 Ti + Pod GGUF RTX PRO 4500, both gens completed, zero reject). The live Pod bug had TWO causes, both fixed: (1) stale engine signal at the workflow swap — now resolved ONCE after refresh in commandExecutor.js; (2) THE REAL ONE — the generated _gguf workflow files carried BOTH unet loaders + baked test media, and ComfyUI eager-validates every node at prompt time → the unselected bf16 loader rejected on a GGUF-only Pod. Fixed in the BUILD SCRIPT generate_ltx.py (one loader per file + placeholder media). Preview_Only tier-2 strip-check noise also cleaned. NEXT = Phase B: migrate deps+workflow to one engines:{local,remote} profile + one resolve(model,op,engine). Offline + parity-tested; no Pod until the swap-retarget smoke.

completed: PHASE A — DONE + LIVE-VERIFIED both engines (commits 66efefe fix+converter+gotcha, cd2883b kanban). (a) commandExecutor.js runCommand: await remoteEngineClient.refresh() (skip when forceLocal) at the TOP of the async body, resolve `const engine = forceLocal ? 'local' : (isRemote() ? 'remote' : 'local')` ONCE, gate the ggufWhenRemote swap on `engine === 'remote'`. Diag removed. (b) THE REAL FIX — generate_ltx.py (comfy_workflows/scripts/workflow_generation/): _select_loader(wf, use_gguf) keeps ONE unet loader per flavour, deletes the other, repoints the dropped loader's Model_Connect (MpiReroute, node 160) MODEL-slot-0 consumer to the kept loader; _stamp_placeholders(wf) resets Input_Start_Frame/Input_End_Frame -> ltx_placeholder.png and Input_Audio_File -> ltx_silence.wav + drops stale audioUI. Both called per flavour in build(). All 8 LTX_*.json regenerated: each has EXACTLY ONE loader (bf16 files UNETLoader, gguf files UnetLoaderGGUF), correct placeholder media, zero test-file refs, zero dangling links. LIVE: local bf16 3m20s + Pod GGUF 2m1s both completed.

Preview_Only tier-2 noise FIXED (commit 5be8dae): comfyController.js strip-check recognised only the tier-1 node title 'preview_only'; LTX/WAN video workflows use tier-2 'Input_Preview_Only' (MPI-127) and _buildParams dual-emits BOTH param keys, so the check false-fired on EVERY stage-1 video gen (warn + strip the already-injected boolean). Now accepts EITHER title, warns+strips only when NEITHER exists (genuine: _stage2 sibling has no preview node), strips both keys. Logic-verified (gate self-check all tiers + stage-2). NOT live-confirmed — next gen's console should no longer print the warning.
Investigation map preserved at research/investigation-summary.md; engine-split WORKFLOW-axis gotcha added to docs/gotchas.md (after the deps-axis gotcha ~line 121).
pending: PHASE B (PRIMARY NEXT — offline, parity-tested, NO Pod to start): (1) Add engines:{local,remote} block to LTX in js/data/modelConstants/models.js — `local:{extraDeps:[...bf16...], workflowSuffix:''}`, `remote:{extraDeps:[...gguf..., ComfyUI-GGUF], workflowSuffix:'_gguf'}`; KEEP legacy localDeps/remoteDeps/ggufWhenRemote temporarily. (2) In resolveModelDeps.js, make engineDepsOf read model.engines?.[engine]?.extraDeps (fallback to legacy localDeps/remoteDeps during migration). Add resolveWorkflowFile(model, op, engine, {stage2}) returning workflows[op] + (_stage2?) + engines[engine].workflowSuffix (suffix order MUST yield ..._stage2_gguf.json). Add thin resolve(model, selectedOps, engine, {stage2, op}) -> {depIds, workflowFile, nodeIds}. Keep resolveDeps/resolveFullUniverse/deriveInstalledOps working (same engineDepsOf). (3) Point the workflow swap in commandExecutor.js at resolveWorkflowFile(...) instead of _toGgufFilename/ggufWhenRemote. TESTS (tests/resolve-model-deps.test.cjs, keep node/DOM-free, currently 10/10): parity (engines block == legacy both engines), workflow filenames (LTX_t2v.json / LTX_t2v_gguf.json / LTX_t2v_stage2_gguf.json), AND a synthetic op-keyed+engine-split fixture proving the two axes UNION (engineDepsOf currently IGNORES operations — a LATENT bug Phase B must fix + test). Verify mode = auto (parity tests); smoke the swap-retarget before Phase C.

PHASE C (after B proven): delete localDeps/remoteDeps/ggufWhenRemote/_toGgufFilename + legacy fallbacks; fix residual union risk _confirmWholeUninstall (MpiModelManager:241) + latent _opUninstallDepIds (:221-222) + MpiPromptBox:137; rewrite the STALE .claude/rules/comfy_engine.md (NEEDS USER PERMISSION — CLAUDE.md rule); update docs/gotchas.md engine-split entries + feedback_engine_split_sweep_all_consumers memory to the single-resolver invariant. Final live re-verify both engines.

FOLLOW-UPS (no new cards — user explicitly wants FEWER cards; fold into Phase B/C or note): (a) DEPS install-check reads engine signal while CONNECTING — isModelUsable (modelRegistry.js:259 reads isRemote() live) demanded the gguf set LOCALLY while the Pod was still downloading -> false 'ltx-23 not installed — cannot reuse full prompt' toast. This is the Phase B/C deps-axis smear; the connecting-state behaviour (local-truth vs remote-intent) is a PRODUCT call the user owns. GOOD REPRO the user wants kept: move the local gguf file OUT of its folder -> app should still report local bf16 usable. (b) MPI-164 (verify-bar at ~95%) stays its OWN existing card — untouched, separate.

next_action.description: Implement MPI-165 Phase B — migrate deps + workflow to ONE engines:{local,remote} profile block + ONE resolver. Read the plan (.agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-165/plan.md Phase B section) + validation.md FIRST. STEPS: (1) add engines:{local:{extraDeps,workflowSuffix:''}, remote:{extraDeps,workflowSuffix:'_gguf'}} to the LTX entry in js/data/modelConstants/models.js, KEEPING legacy localDeps/remoteDeps/ggufWhenRemote for now; (2) in js/data/modelConstants/resolveModelDeps.js make engineDepsOf read model.engines?.[engine]?.extraDeps (fallback to legacy), add resolveWorkflowFile(model,op,engine,{stage2}) (= workflows[op] + _stage2? + engines[engine].workflowSuffix — order must give ..._stage2_gguf.json) and a thin resolve(model,ops,engine,{stage2,op}) -> {depIds,workflowFile,nodeIds}; (3) retarget the workflow swap in js/services/commandExecutor.js to resolveWorkflowFile instead of _toGgufFilename/ggufWhenRemote. ADD TESTS to tests/resolve-model-deps.test.cjs (keep node/DOM-free): legacy-vs-engines parity for both engines, the 3 workflow filenames, AND a synthetic op-keyed+engine-split fixture proving the operation axis and engine axis UNION (engineDepsOf currently IGNORES operations — fix that latent bug here). Verify mode = auto (parity tests pass); smoke the swap-retarget. NO Pod needed to build/test Phase B; only the final live re-verify (Phase C) needs one (user spins it, never autonomous). Brief the user before writing code (mpi-continue gate). Shared git tree: commit by explicit pathspec, never git add .; push is user-only. DO NOT create new cards — the user explicitly wants fewer; fold follow-ups into B/C.

key_decisions: THE REAL POD-REJECT BUG was NOT just the engine signal (Phase A's original hypothesis) — it was the GENERATED WORKFLOW FILE CONTENT: the _gguf files carried BOTH unet loaders (an MpiIfElse picks at runtime, but ComfyUI EAGER-VALIDATES every node's file inputs at PROMPT time regardless of lazy wiring → the unselected bf16 loader rejected on a GGUF-only Pod) + the template's baked test media. Lazy-eval defers EXECUTION, not VALIDATION. Proven live by the user (removed local gguf file → ComfyUI errored even though MpiIfElse never pulls it).

FIX LIVES IN THE BUILD SCRIPT, not runtime: the template now wires ONLY the bf16 loader and PARKS the GGUF one (user's deliberate save convention — makes the splice point unambiguous). generate_ltx.py _select_loader() emits one-loader-per-file; _stamp_placeholders() stamps engine-staged placeholder media. This is the right layer — a generated workflow must reference only weights/media that exist on the TARGET engine.

Same loader-swap pattern will extend to DualCLIPLoader when quantized Gemma is tested. If quantized Gemma passes, GGUF may become the SINGLE dep for both engines, retiring the whole bf16/GGUF split. _select_loader is title-driven + generic enough to reuse.

Mixed-tier is INTENTIONAL: image workflows use tier-1 bare titles (Preview_Only), video workflows (LTX/WAN) use tier-2 Input_* (Input_Preview_Only); _buildParams dual-emits Input_<key> for every bare key so injection serves both. The .claude/rules/comfy_injection.md ALREADY documents both tiers (lines 123, 126) — the rule was correct, only the comfyController strip-check code was stale.

Resolve-engine-ONCE is the core principle: resolve a concrete 'local'|'remote' string ONCE per gen AFTER refresh(), then thread it. NEVER let each consumer call isRemote() independently (a read milliseconds before vs after refresh disagrees — that smear caused every half-wire). Phase B makes this structural via the one resolver.
constraints: Shared git tree — commit by explicit pathspec, never git add . / -A. Push is user-only. mpi-ci is a SEPARATE repo.
resolveModelDeps.js MUST stay browser/DOM-free (node-unit-testable via tests/resolve-model-deps.test.cjs; 10/10 pass).

Suffix order MUST yield ..._stage2_gguf.json (workflowSuffix applied AFTER _stage2), matching generate_ltx.py output. The _gguf workflow files already exist on disk (regenerated this session) and use the single correct loader.

engineDepsOf currently IGNORES the operation axis (Wan commonDeps + operations{}) — Phase B must make engineDepsOf compose BOTH axes (union) and TEST it with a synthetic op-keyed+engine-split fixture. The two axes are orthogonal: a model may have neither/one/both.
.claude/rules/comfy_engine.md is STALE (never mentions engine-split/the swap/the converter) — rewriting it (Phase C) needs explicit user permission per CLAUDE.md.
Pod create/delete is NEVER autonomous — the user spins/kills it. NO Pod is needed for Phase B (offline parity tests). Pod stopped this session to save money.
DO NOT create new kanban cards — the user explicitly wants FEWER cards, not more. Fold follow-ups into Phase B/C or note in validation.md.
Caveman + ponytail output modes active (terse prose, minimal solutions) — cosmetic.

js/data/modelConstants/resolveModelDeps.js (engineDepsOf, resolveDeps, resolveFullUniverse, deriveInstalledOps — the resolver to extend)
tests/resolve-model-deps.test.cjs (10/10 — extend with parity + the synthetic op-keyed+engine-split fixture)
js/services/commandExecutor.js (runCommand ~844; the swap ~858-890 now resolves engine once + uses _toGgufFilename — retarget to resolveWorkflowFile in Phase B)
comfy_workflows/scripts/workflow_generation/generate_ltx.py (the converter — _select_loader + _stamp_placeholders; reference for the suffix/name contract)

rules_active: docs/gotchas.md (engine-split deps + WORKFLOW axis entries + LTX-2.3 workflow authoring section)
resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/5e9fb5b9-de95-4824-9eb4-7d5e93d04294.json and use mpi-continue. We are on branch RunPod implementing MPI-165 (engine-variant consolidation). PHASE A is DONE + LIVE-VERIFIED on BOTH engines (local bf16 RTX 4060 Ti 3m20s + Pod GGUF RTX PRO 4500 2m1s, both gens completed, zero reject — commits 66efefe + cd2883b). IMPORTANT CORRECTION captured this session: the real Pod-reject bug was NOT only the engine signal — the GENERATED _gguf workflow files carried BOTH unet loaders + the template's baked test media, and ComfyUI EAGER-VALIDATES every node at prompt time (lazy MpiIfElse defers execution, not validation), so the unselected bf16 loader rejected on a GGUF-only Pod. The fix lives in the BUILD SCRIPT generate_ltx.py: _select_loader() keeps one loader per file (repoints Model_Connect), _stamp_placeholders() stamps ltx_placeholder.png / ltx_silence.wav. Also fixed the Preview_Only tier-2 strip-check noise (commit 5be8dae). NEXT = PHASE B (offline, parity-tested, NO Pod needed to start): migrate deps+workflow to ONE engines:{local,remote} profile block (extraDeps + workflowSuffix) + ONE resolve(model,op,engine) -> {deps,workflow,nodes}; add resolveWorkflowFile + make engineDepsOf read engines[engine].extraDeps (keep legacy localDeps/remoteDeps/ggufWhenRemote as temporary fallback, deleted in Phase C); retarget the commandExecutor workflow swap to resolveWorkflowFile. Add tests to tests/resolve-model-deps.test.cjs: legacy-vs-engines parity both engines, the 3 workflow filenames, AND a synthetic op-keyed+engine-split fixture proving the operation axis and engine axis UNION (engineDepsOf currently IGNORES operations — a latent bug Phase B must fix). resolveModelDeps.js must stay browser/DOM-free. Brief me before writing code. Shared git tree: explicit pathspec only, never git add .; push is user-only. DO NOT create new kanban cards — I want FEWER cards; fold the two open follow-ups (the connecting-state deps toast, repro = move local gguf file out of its folder; and MPI-164 which stays its own card) into Phase B/C or notes. Pod is stopped — only Phase C's final live re-verify needs one (I spin it).
